message:
chore(beads): reopen q0y8h — fcp-e2e package test build regression reappeared

Re-opens `flywheel_connectors-q0y8h` from `closed` back to
`in_progress`. The original fix (commit f8712801, "dropped const from
is_secret_mutation/is_system_namespace/is_shell_binary/approval_for_
operation in connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs") closed the
specific `const fn` str-match compile errors in fcp-kubernetes, but the
broader symptom ("`fcp-e2e` package test builds fail") has resurfaced
under the current toolchain and needs fresh triage before the bead
can be closed again.

No other issues touched in this delta — single-line status flip on
the one row. Bead hygiene only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1940,7 +1940,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pwff.2","title":"fcp.zendesk: Provisioning Automation (OAuth/API token, webhooks)","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Provisioning Automation (OAuth/API token, webhooks)

## Goal
Automate Zendesk onboarding with minimal human steps while keeping secrets zone-scoped.

## Requirements
- Support auth via `CredentialId`:
  - OAuth2 (preferred)
  - API token (policy-gated)
- Subdomain binding and validation.
- Webhook setup automation:
  - register event webhooks (ticket created/updated, SLA warnings)
  - validate webhook secret/signature configuration
- Validate NetworkConstraints pinning.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness without leaking secrets.
- Fully mockable for unit tests.

Added ZendeskAuth enum (Token{subdomain,email,api_token} / CredentialId{subdomain,credential_id}) with Clone, custom Debug (redacted), redacted_label(), is_secretless(), subdomain(). Updated ZendeskClient with new_with_auth(), health_check() via /users/me.json, manual Debug impl with finish_non_exhaustive(). Added ZendeskConfig with strict from_params() xor validation. Added handle_doctor() (6 checks) and handle_self_check() with SelfCheckReport. Updated handle_configure/handle_health. Added doctor+self_check dispatch in main.rs. 58 tests pass (34 unit + 24 integration), 0 clippy warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-02T20:13:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pwff.3","title":"TEST: Zendesk Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: Zendesk Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)

## Goal
Mock-only tests for fcp.zendesk (ticket CRUD, customer lookup, KB access, macros, webhooks).

## Requirements
- No real Zendesk calls in CI.
- Mock endpoints for:
  - ticket CRUD + search
  - user/customer lookup
  - KB search/article fetch
  - macro list/apply
  - webhook ingestion
- Tests for:
  - attachment host pinning and size limits
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - capability gating for writes and admin ops
  - taint handling for incoming webhooks

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic CI suite.
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

Coverage: 9 happy-path ops (create_ticket, get_ticket, update_ticket, delete_ticket, search_tickets, list_ticket_comments, search_articles, get_article, apply_macro), 4 error taxonomy (401/404/429/500), 3 FCP2 default-deny, 4 lifecycle, 4 input validation. All 45 tests pass (21 inline + 24 integration), clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-03-02T10:54:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pwff.4","title":"E2E: Zendesk Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Zendesk Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Run the shared Mechanical Connector Compliance Runner against fcp.zendesk.

## Must verify
- Default deny via CapabilityTokens.
- NetworkConstraints enforcement.
- Receipts/audit for write/dangerous ops (ticket updates, macro applies if policy treats as write).
- Taint-aware webhook ingestion.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner output bundle is green for fcp.zendesk.

All 4 tests pass, clippy clean. Tests executed on remote worker due to local binary execution issue.","created_at":"2026-03-02T21:47:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-q0y8h","title":"fcp-kubernetes: const fn str matches break fcp-e2e package test builds","description":"**File**: connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs:1424-1465, 2964-2985

**Symptom**: The user-requested verification command `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p3-r1 rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-conformance -p fcp-e2e` fails before reaching the touched tests because `fcp-e2e` pulls `fcp-kubernetes` as a dev-dependency and `fcp-kubernetes` no longer compiles on the current nightly.

**Compiler errors**:
```
error[E0658]: cannot match on `str` in constant functions
  --> connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs:1424:9
error: `PartialEq` is not yet stable as a const trait
  --> connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs:1424:9
...
error: could not compile `fcp-kubernetes` (lib) due to 102 previous errors
```

**Root cause**: multiple `const fn` bodies use `matches!` over `&str` literals. On the current toolchain, `str` equality inside `const fn` still requires unstable const-trait support, so these match expressions are rejected.

**Why it matters**: unrelated `fcp-e2e` verification lanes are blocked by a connector compile failure outside their test target.

**Fix plan**: replace the `matches!`-based string switches in those `const fn` helpers with non-const helpers, or rewrite them to avoid `&str` pattern matching in `const fn` entirely. Then add a focused compile/test lane that exercises `fcp-kubernetes` from the `fcp-e2e` package boundary so this regression is caught earlier.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:03:10.514679Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:34.101890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:28.808861Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","connectors","kubernetes"],"comments":[{"id":2812,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q0y8h","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed commit f8712801: dropped const from is_secret_mutation, is_system_namespace, is_shell_binary, approval_for_operation in connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs. item_type_name kept as const fn (matches on enum variants, not str). Verified: cargo build -p fcp-kubernetes clean; cargo test -p fcp-kubernetes --lib 514/514 pass; cargo build -p fcp-e2e --tests compiles clean. Unblocks P4 + fcp-e2e verification lanes.","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:34Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-q0y8h","title":"fcp-kubernetes: const fn str matches break fcp-e2e package test builds","description":"**File**: connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs:1424-1465, 2964-2985

**Symptom**: The user-requested verification command `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p3-r1 rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-conformance -p fcp-e2e` fails before reaching the touched tests because `fcp-e2e` pulls `fcp-kubernetes` as a dev-dependency and `fcp-kubernetes` no longer compiles on the current nightly.

**Compiler errors**:
```
error[E0658]: cannot match on `str` in constant functions
  --> connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs:1424:9
error: `PartialEq` is not yet stable as a const trait
  --> connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs:1424:9
...
error: could not compile `fcp-kubernetes` (lib) due to 102 previous errors
```

**Root cause**: multiple `const fn` bodies use `matches!` over `&str` literals. On the current toolchain, `str` equality inside `const fn` still requires unstable const-trait support, so these match expressions are rejected.

**Why it matters**: unrelated `fcp-e2e` verification lanes are blocked by a connector compile failure outside their test target.

**Fix plan**: replace the `matches!`-based string switches in those `const fn` helpers with non-const helpers, or rewrite them to avoid `&str` pattern matching in `const fn` entirely. Then add a focused compile/test lane that exercises `fcp-kubernetes` from the `fcp-e2e` package boundary so this regression is caught earlier.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:03:10.514679Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:00:41.264621Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","connectors","kubernetes"],"comments":[{"id":2812,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q0y8h","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed commit f8712801: dropped const from is_secret_mutation, is_system_namespace, is_shell_binary, approval_for_operation in connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs. item_type_name kept as const fn (matches on enum variants, not str). Verified: cargo build -p fcp-kubernetes clean; cargo test -p fcp-kubernetes --lib 514/514 pass; cargo build -p fcp-e2e --tests compiles clean. Unblocks P4 + fcp-e2e verification lanes.","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-q10z","title":"TEST: Trust/Quorum Model Unit Tests (n/f Assumptions, Threshold Rules, Degraded Mode)","description":"# TEST: Trust/Quorum Model Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate trust/quorum and degraded-mode policy rules so the mesh behaves predictably under partial failure/byzantine assumptions.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for Byzantine fault tolerance model and quorum rules.

## Test Categories

### n/f Assumptions
- [ ] 3-node mesh tolerates 0 Byzantine faults
- [ ] 4-node mesh tolerates 1 Byzantine fault
- [ ] 7-node mesh tolerates 2 Byzantine faults
- [ ] f < n/3 invariant enforced
- [ ] Quorum size = n - f calculated correctly

### Threshold Rules
- [ ] Zone operations require 2f+1 signatures
- [ ] Checkpoint advancement requires quorum
- [ ] Key rotation requires quorum
- [ ] Revocation propagation requires quorum
- [ ] Threshold signature verification

### Degraded Mode Semantics
- [ ] Degraded mode triggers at f failures
- [ ] Operations pause when quorum lost
- [ ] Clear error codes for degraded state
- [ ] Recovery path when nodes return
- [ ] Degraded mode logged/alerted

### Safety Tier Quorums
- [ ] Safe operations: single node
- [ ] Risky operations: 2f+1 quorum
- [ ] Dangerous operations: higher quorum
- [ ] Critical operations: owner quorum

### Split-Brain Prevention
- [ ] Only one partition can make progress
- [ ] Minority partition refuses writes
- [ ] Majority partition continues
- [ ] Merge after heal

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/quorum/`
- `quorum_3_of_3.cbor`
- `quorum_3_of_5.cbor`
- `degraded_state.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_quorum_threshold",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "n": 5,
  "f": 1,
  "quorum_size": 4,
  "signatures_collected": 4,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quorum calculations are correct
- Degraded mode is reliable
- Split-brain is prevented

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","title":"[FCP3/P2.2] Carve fcp-policy as the single owner of zone, capability, and provenance semantics","description":"Background: zone binding, capability checks, provenance, and admission logic are central platform semantics, but today they are still partly smeared between shared core code and host owned orchestration paths. That makes policy too easy to bypass or reinterpret.

Scope: define and extract fcp-policy as the owner of security and admission semantics: zone rules, capability scoping, provenance constraints, placement eligibility inputs, and any policy relevant decision vocabulary that should be shared across host, mesh, and CLI explanation flows. Include canonical policy decision records, denial reasons, and deterministic policy fixtures so later negative tests, placement checks, and CLI explanation flows can prove semantics mechanically instead of recreating them ad hoc.

Acceptance: policy concepts have a single owner, downstream code consumes those policy contracts rather than embedding parallel policy logic, and policy outcomes are explicit enough for negative tests and operator explanations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:04.398443Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43.952352Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43.952119Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-2","policy","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:21.133113Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xn3h3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:21.400710Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2073,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak: Created fcp-policy crate (commit 2493cbce). Phase 1 re-exports: 60+ types across 8 domains (zone, capabilities, identity, provenance, approval, policy engine, diffs, resources). 7 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:53:26Z"},{"id":2076,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Created crates/fcp-policy/ with 151-line facade re-exporting policy types from fcp-core. Covers: CapabilityToken/Id/Grant/Verifier, ZoneId/ZoneDefinition/ZonePolicy, PolicyEngine/PolicyDecision/PolicyBundle, Provenance/TaintLevel/IntegrityLevel, ApprovalToken, RoleGraph, Secret management, PostureRequirements, Supply chain verification. Tests verify type accessibility.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","title":"[FCP3/P3] Realign FCPC and FCPS wire and durable truth around mesh native semantics","description":"Background: V3 distinguishes a live control and evidence plane, FCPC, from a durable object plane, FCPS. Today the implementation still leans too heavily on host local runtime state and adapter specific behavior, especially around admin and control truth and durable evidence objects.

Scope: make FCPC and FCPS canonical, define durable schemas for the facts the system must remember beyond a single host process, and align store, manifest, registry, and conformance work with that model. The phase must make wire contracts and durable object shapes replayable and inspectable through deterministic fixtures, serialization round trips, repair scenarios, cross crate integration tests, and structured diagnostic artifacts so later user facing tooling can explain what is live, what is durable, and how the two relate. This phase is successful when durable truth can be replayed, repaired, and explained without treating the host process as the sole authority.

Acceptance: FCPC and FCPS semantics are backed by stable schemas, proof fixtures, and detailed transcripts or logs that later conformance, operator, and e2e beads can reuse directly instead of reconstructing from host local behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01.411236Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:59.715602Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:59.386420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-3","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.499616Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.117871Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.308760Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.698077Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vqfld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:11.921808Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2050,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"HTTP admin, local process memory, and ad hoc host structs are adapters in this phase, not the semantic model. If a fact matters for readiness, failover, or audit, it should have a canonical FCPC or FCPS form rather than living only in host local code paths.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01Z"},{"id":2367,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","author":"jemanuel","text":"All 5 children closed (P3.1-P3.5). FCPC/FCPS wire and durable truth fully realigned around mesh-native semantics. 921 conformance vectors, 13 FCPS durability boundary tests, comprehensive store/repair/GC coverage.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:59Z"}]}
